page: Microsoft Azure EC16es_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the ECEV5 server family. It is built on the x86_64 architecture and features 16 dedicated vCPUs running on Intel processors. The instance is provisioned with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. Virtualization is managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

This server configuration does not include any local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met using external volumes, though the instance is designated as Premium Storage capable. Additionally, the server does not feature any graphics processing units and includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the EC16es_v5 offers a high memory density relative to its compute resources, making it a structured option for memory-intensive workloads that do not require local disk space or hardware acceleration. It is designed for workloads such as relational databases, in-memory caching systems, and enterprise applications that benefit from a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio.
description: Microsoft Azure EC16es_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the ECEV5 family, featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs on an x86_64 Intel processor architecture. Managed by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance provides 128.0 GB of RAM, establishing an 8.0 GB memory-to-core ratio. The server does not include local storage, relying instead on its Premium Storage capability for external disk attachments, and lacks integrated GPUs. With zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses, it is designed for secure, memory-intensive workloads. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio makes this instance suitable for database management systems, in-memory data caching, and enterprise applications requiring substantial memory capacity without the need for local physical disks or hardware acceleration.
